title: Why Indian Schools Are Failing Their Best Teachers
description: A deep dive into teacher attrition, the IB-CBSE partnership, and why student happiness should be the only metric that matters. Mahesh argues that most school reform ignores the one variable that changes everything.

title: Building EdTech That Actually Works for Bharat
description: Priya explains why most EdTech products are built for urban English-speakers and how her startup is different — vernacular-first, offline-capable, and designed around the constraints of a government school teacher.

title: NEP 2020: Promise vs. Reality on the Ground
description: Three years after NEP was announced, what has actually changed in classrooms? Rohan, a former government advisor who helped draft the policy, gives an unvarnished assessment of what landed and what didn't.

title: From IIT to Villages: The Entrepreneurship No One Talks About
description: Kavitha left a Goldman Sachs role to build a supply chain startup for rural artisans. Her framework for validating ideas in markets with zero data — and zero safety net — is one of the most practical things I've heard.

title: Why India's Higher Education Rankings Game Is Broken
description: The former VC of a top-ranked university explains what the NIRF rankings actually measure, what they miss entirely, and why the institutions winning the game are often the ones doing the least interesting work.

title: The Policy Bottleneck Killing Indian Startups
description: A policy researcher at a Delhi think tank breaks down the three regulatory knots strangling early-stage startups — and proposes concrete reforms that wouldn't require a single rupee of additional budget.

title: Skill India at 10: What Worked, What Flopped
description: The architect of a national skill development program does a candid post-mortem — including the decisions he now regrets — and maps what version 2.0 must look like if it's going to move the needle on employment.

title: Raising a Series A in India vs. Silicon Valley: The Brutal Truth
description: Nisha closed both an Indian and a US round in the same year. Her candid comparison of investor behaviour, term sheets, and the sheer difference in due diligence culture will make you rethink the fundraising playbook.
